Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, low total CO₂, significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria, with low urine specific gravity. These findings suggest impaired renal function, possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, and metabolic acidosis. The low HbA1c is unusual given glycosuria and may reflect anemia or recent changes in glycemic control. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may indicate underlying urinary tract pathology. Recommend further nephrological and metabolic evaluation.
